site_tagline: A faithful FORTRAN-66 / DEC FORTRAN-10 interpreter, in Python.
site_description: forterp runs FORTRAN against a pluggable machine value model and a pluggable front-end dialect: strict ANSI FORTRAN-66 by default, the DEC FORTRAN-10 superset on demand — on a portable 64-bit machine or a faithful 36-bit PDP-10. Extracted from an interpreter built to run the 1978 games DECWAR and Empire unmodified.
attribution: Software Architecture, Design & Engineering by Nicholas J. Kisseberth.
Code Synthesized via Anthropic Claude Code / Opus 4.8.
Code Review by OpenAI Codex / ChatGPT 5.5.

docs.title: Language reference
docs.summary: The FORTRAN dialect forterp implements: standard FORTRAN-66 plus the DEC FORTRAN-10 extensions, written for users of the interpreter — with the deliberate divergences spelled out.

docs.title: Architecture & design
docs.summary: How it works inside and why: the source → lexer → parser → engine pipeline, the pluggable Target value model and Dialect axis, the memory/control models, and the seams that make it standalone.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.summary: Dated history of the standalone interpreter, in order: the target/dialect axes, the CLI and monitor, the conformance work, and the review sweep.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.summary: Index of the forterp documentation and the authoritative standards it targets.
